summary: Skill created 2026-01-26, never committed to git, processor.py uses inline prompts and never referenced canonical prompt files

timeline.event: 13 legacy meeting prompts deleted per references/legacy_prompts.md

root_cause: processor.py was written from scratch with inline BLOCK_DEFINITIONS hardcoded in the script, completely bypassing the canonical Prompts/Blocks/ files

evidence.inline_prompts_in_processor: BLOCK_DEFINITIONS dict hardcoded in processor.py lines 44-59
evidence.canonical_prompts_unused: 37 prompt files exist in Prompts/Blocks/ but none referenced by processor.py
evidence.legacy_reference_doc: references/legacy_prompts.md documents 13 deleted prompt files from con_TRpqstPn1qgxSiAb

used_by_other_scripts: N5/workers/worker_generate_blocks.py loads all prompts from Prompts/Blocks/
generate_blocks.py (root) maps 11 block codes to prompt files

gap_analysis.canonical_system_exists: worker_generate_blocks.py and generate_blocks.py properly load prompt files
gap_analysis.skill_bypassed_canonical: meeting-ingestion skill reimplemented prompts as hardcoded strings
gap_analysis.no_cross_reference: processor.py does not import or reference any existing prompt loading logic
gap_analysis.parallel_implementations: Two different approaches to same intelligence blocks exist simultaneously
